scan.access_pattern: sequential contiguous row-range scan over all lineitem rows; inline l_shipdate cutoff check in the hot loop; no zone-map block classification or atomic morsel scheduling

data_structures.dim_filter: none; direct inline comparison on l_shipdate[row] <= 10471
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local direct_array(4 slots) with shared dense_key_lut[65536] mapping (l_returnflag<<8)|l_linestatus for AF,NF,NO,RF to slot ids; merge exactly 4 slots per thread

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current plan assumes shipdate zone-map pruning will help, but all 600 lineitem shipdate blocks are boundary blocks, so the runtime still performs a full 59.99M-row scan while paying extra block classification, atomic block scheduling, and per-row compact-hash probes to produce only 4 groups. Strategy category A/D: remove the ineffective zone-map-driven accept/boundary path from the hot loop, scan contiguous row ranges directly with the l_shipdate <= 10471 predicate inline, map the four observed (l_returnflag,l_linestatus) combinations to dense ids with a tiny lookup table, aggregate into a 4-slot thread-local direct array that fits in L1, then merge 4 slots per thread and sort the 4 output rows.

correctness_anchors.sum_disc_price_scaled_formula: accumulate l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount); final output divides by 10000
correctness_anchors.sum_charge_scaled_formula: accumulate l_extendedprice * (100 - l_discount) * (100 + l_tax); final output divides by 1000000
